reasoning: The question asks whether 'Strategic developments' in Ivory Coast in the 30 days before the resolution date (implied as 2026-01-24) will exceed the 30-day average over the 360 days before 2025-10-26 (the date of the 2025 elections). The baseline average is ~4.75 events per month. Historically, for intermittent conflict event counts per month, the distribution is right-skewed, meaning the median is lower than the mean. Thus, a randomly selected month has a <50% chance of exceeding the prior 12-month mean. Furthermore, the 12 months preceding October 2025 will be a pre-election period characterized by political maneuvering, which likely heightens the event baseline. By January 2026, the election will have concluded. Barring a prolonged post-election crisis (like 2010-2011, which is possible but not the most likely scenario currently given the country's recent stability and political realignments), the typical post-election period and January holidays often see a reversion to quieter political environments, reducing the count. Therefore, the probability of exceeding the elevated pre-election mean is estimated at slightly below 50%.
